failure_surface: Multiple consecutive SyntaxError exceptions during code execution, indicating improperly formatted code submission prior to successful execution after retry.
root_cause: Agent submitted code with malformed function/tool call formatting that caused parsing failures. The tool execution interface received corrupted or incomplete code blocks, leading to repeated 'invalid syntax' errors before eventually accepting a clean code submission.
minimal_fix_hypothesis: Before executing code through tool calls, agent should ensure raw code content contains no extraneous characters, markdown delimiters embedded incorrectly, or truncated sections that would cause syntax parsing errors in the execution environment.
evidence: First three tool execution attempts returned identical 'SyntaxError: invalid syntax' on line 1
Error message showed 'Cell In[1], line 1\n     <tool_call>' indicating tool call wrapper leaked into code content
Fourth submission explicitly stated 'submit without any markdown or additional context', after which execution succeeded
Final successful output confirmed 'Output saved successfully' indicating code logic itself was correct

memory_items.title: Clean Code Block Submission
memory_items.lesson: When submitting code to execution tools, strip all markdown delimiters, wrapper artifacts, and extraneous text to avoid syntax parsing errors.
memory_items.generalization: Ensure raw code passed to executors contains only valid Python statements without tool-call framing leakage.

memory_items.title: Pre-Execution Validation Check
memory_items.lesson: Validate that code can be syntactically parsed before sending to execution tools, especially after generating complex multi-block responses.
memory_items.generalization: Perform basic syntax verification to catch formatting issues that would otherwise waste execution attempts.

memory_items.title: Iterative Retry Strategy
memory_items.lesson: After repeated execution failures with identical errors, simplify the submission format to isolate whether the issue is code logic or submission formatting.
memory_items.generalization: Distinguish between implementation bugs and tool-interaction bugs by changing submission format on retry.

edits.content:
## 7. Tool Execution Hygiene
- **Code Submission Format**: Ensure raw code sent to execution tools contains only valid Python without embedded markdown, tool wrappers, or truncation artifacts.
- **Validation Before Send**: Verify syntax correctness programmatically or mentally before execution to avoid repeated parse failures.
- **Retry Differentiation**: On repeated 'SyntaxError' with identical line numbers, suspect submission corruption rather than logic error; clean up formatting on retry.
- **Environment Compatibility**: Code should be standalone executable - include all necessary imports and avoid reliance on external state.
